title: UP govt drops electricity purchase from Adani Power: Company charged additional 75 paise per unit from cons... - Bhaskar English
content:
- English News
- Local
- Uttar pradesh
- UP Govt Drops Electricity Purchase From Adani Power: Company Charged Additional 75 Paise Per Unit From Consumers; GST Relief Also Denied
UP govt drops electricity purchase from Adani Power:Company charged additional 75 paise per unit from consumers; also denied GST relief
- Copy link
The Uttar Pradesh Electricity Regulatory Commission (UPERC) has put an immediate stop to the proposal to purchase 1500 MW of electricity from Adani Power, citing significant discrepancies in fixed and variable charges.
The Commission found that by adding the cost of the FGD (Flue Gas Desulfurisation) plant, recovery was being made at higher rates—ranging from 55 to 75 paise per unit. It also noted that reductions linked to the revised GST rates on coal had not been implemented.
FGD cost added despite withdrawal of mandatory order
According to UPERC, Adani Power continued to add the cost of installing an FGD plant into the power purchase rate, despite the Government of India withdrawing its earlier order mandating FGD installation in all thermal power plants. As a result, the per-unit cost remained inflated.
The Commission has now made Adani Power a party to the case and directed the Power Corporation to submit a revised proposal with reduced rates if the company fails to install the FGD plant by December 18.
What FGD is & why it matters?
FGD (Flue Gas Desulfurisation) is an industrial method used to remove sulfur dioxide (SO₂) from emissions produced by coal-fired power plants. SO₂ contributes to acid rain and air pollution.
The process uses absorbents like lime or limestone to reduce emissions.Despite this, the Commission stated that charging consumers for FGD installation when it is not being implemented, and is no longer mandatory, is unjustified.
Key questions raised by the commission
UPERC has asked the Power Corporation to explain:
- Why the impact of reduced GST on coal was not reflected in the electricity tariff.
- Why fixed cost, variable cost, and the 1.2% auxiliary consumption charges were not reduced due to non-installation of FGD.
- Why recalculation of reduced energy consumption was not carried out.
- Why an agreement was made at the high rate of ₹5.38 per unit without proper assessment when the removal of FGD cost and reduction in GST should have naturally brought down the tariff by 55–75 paise.
Power struggle committee targets grant thornton
The Uttar Pradesh Bijli Sangharsh Samiti has levelled serious allegations in the matter. Coordinator Shailendra Dubey accused Grant Thornton, the consultant for Adani Power, of preparing 'fake RFPs' during the earlier privatisation process for Purvanchal and Dakshinanchal discoms.
He alleged that the same consultancy firm is now involved in efforts to “loot consumers” through inflated power purchase rates. The committee has demanded that all privatization-related documents prepared by Grant Thornton be cancelled and the privatization process of power corporations be withdrawn.
Consumer council flags 11,000+ pending complaints
The Consumer Council has opposed the non-payment of compensation to consumers under the Standard of Performance Regulation-2019. Under the rules of the Electricity Regulatory Commission, consumers must receive compensation if complaints registered on helpline 1912 are not resolved within the prescribed timeframe.
Of the complaints filed on 1912, 11,494 remain pending — including 3,504 linked to smart meters. In Lucknow alone, where the vertical structure system is in place, 1,075 complaints are pending, with smart meter issues forming the majority.
Breakdown of Pending Complaints
- Smart meter: 3,504
- Supply-related: 666
- Billing: 3,332
- Meter-related (other): 2,135
- Information-related: 629
- Others: 837
- Service-related: 391
In Lucknow, pending complaints include:
- Janakipuram Zone: 155 (97 smart meter-related)
- Amausi Zone: 403 (295 smart meter-related)
- Lucknow Central: 241 (224 smart meter-related)
- Gomti Nagar Zone: 279 (222 smart meter-related)
Demand for compensation and OTP verification
Consumer Council President Awadesh Verma said the Regulatory Commission should direct immediate payment of compensation in cases where timelines are not met.
He also questioned why the 1912 helpline has not yet been linked with OTP-based verification, despite repeated instances of 'fake closures' of complaints.The Commission had earlier directed implementation of OTP verification for complaint closure, but it has not been enforced to date.
